1.texto_original: Roboão foi a Siquém, pois todo o Israel se congregara ali para fazê-lo rei.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of 2 Crônicas 10:1

"Roboão foi a Siquém, pois todo o Israel se congregara ali para fazê-lo rei."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 2 Crônicas 10:1

Philological Analysis

"Roboão foi a Siquém, pois todo o Israel se congregara ali para fazê-lo rei."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Roboão foi a Siquém, pois todo o Israel se congregara ali para fazê-lo rei."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"falou-lhes conforme o conselho dos jovens, dizendo: Meu pai fez pesado o vosso jugo, mas eu lhe acrescentarei mais; meu pai vos castigou com açoites, mas eu vos castigarei com escorpiões."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

15.texto_original: O rei, pois, não deu ouvidos ao povo; porque esta mudança vinha de Deus, para que o Senhor confirmasse a sua palavra, a qual falara por intermédio de Aías, o silonita, a Jeroboão, filho de Nabat.
15.normal:
Devotional Study of 2 Crônicas 10:15

"O rei, pois, não deu ouvidos ao povo; porque esta mudança vinha de Deus, para que o Senhor confirmasse a sua palavra, a qual falara por intermédio de Aías, o silonita, a Jeroboão, filho de Nabat."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
15.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 2 Crônicas 10:15

Philological Analysis

"O rei, pois, não deu ouvidos ao povo; porque esta mudança vinha de Deus, para que o Senhor confirmasse a sua palavra, a qual falara por intermédio de Aías, o silonita, a Jeroboão, filho de Nabat."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.

16.texto_original: Vendo, pois, todo o Israel que o rei não lhe dava ouvidos, respondeu-lhe dizendo: Que parte temos nós em David? Não temos herança no filho de Jessé: Cada um as suas tendas, ó Israel! Agora olha por tua casa, ó David! Então todo o Israel se foi para as suas tendas:
16.normal:
Devotional Study of 2 Crônicas 10:16

"Vendo, pois, todo o Israel que o rei não lhe dava ouvidos, respondeu-lhe dizendo: Que parte temos nós em David? Não temos herança no filho de Jessé: Cada um as suas tendas, ó Israel! Agora olha por tua casa, ó David! Então todo o Israel se foi para as suas tendas:"

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

18.texto_original: Então o rei Roboão enviou-lhes Hadorão, que estava sobre a leva de tributários servis; mas os filhos de Israel o apedrejaram, de modo que morreu. E o rei Roboão se apressou a subir para o seu carro, e fugiu para Jerusalém.
18.normal:
Devotional Study of 2 Crônicas 10:18

"Então o rei Roboão enviou-lhes Hadorão, que estava sobre a leva de tributários servis; mas os filhos de Israel o apedrejaram, de modo que morreu. E o rei Roboão se apressou a subir para o seu carro, e fugiu para Jerusalém."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.
